title: Ordered CatBoost specialist on raw strategy categories
summary: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical transformations are designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage, and its docs explicitly discourage manual one-hot preprocessing for rich categorical structure.
rationale: CatBoost’s ordered boosting and categorical transformations are designed to reduce prediction shift from target leakage, and its docs explicitly discourage manual one-hot preprocessing for rich categorical structure. This fits the high-cardinality `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, and their interactions better than the current mostly hand-built thresholds.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost binary model on mostly raw columns plus only a small set of numeric race-state features. Let CatBoost see combinations such as `Driver x Race`, `Race x Compound`, `Compound x Stint`, and `Driver x Compound x Year`; validate with `GroupKFold` on race-year groups only. Blend its OOF predictions with the current tabular model rather than replacing it.
expected_effect: Better capture of driver-, circuit-, and compound-specific priors with less brittle manual feature engineering; likely small but real gain as a complementary specialist.
risk: This overlaps with prior raw-categorical ideas, so the test should be strict: it needs to win as a specialist blend, not just match the current model. Leakage risk remains if encodings or folds mix laps from the same race across train/validation.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
